question_text: Describe the difference between a high-frequency wave and a low-frequency wave.
correct_answer: A high-frequency wave completes many cycles per second, while a low-frequency wave completes fewer cycles per second. High-frequency waves have more waves produced in the same time period than low-frequency waves.
explanation: Frequency is the number of complete waves produced in one second, measured in hertz (Hz). High-frequency waves oscillate rapidly and have many cycles per second (like blue light or high-pitched sounds), while low-frequency waves oscillate more slowly with fewer cycles per second (like red light or low-pitched sounds).
